title: Don't materialize gradients by default

title: Bump version from 0.101.0 to 0.101.1

body: This PR cleans up the interface for forcing with Models. It's inspired by some similar improvements for Breeze.AtmosphereModel. In particular it more clearly distinguishes between "prognostic fields" and "model fields". Model fields (perhaps not the greatest name) is a superset of prognostic fields and may include selected auxiliary fields (but typically not _all_ auxiliary fields) that the model designer deems could be useful for users within forcing and boundary condition functions.

To clear the depot:
If you ever need to clear the depot (e.g. if some packages get corrupted and can't be correctly accessed), you can go to the [Clear Depot pipeline](https://buildkite.com/clima/clear-depot), click "new build", and put your pipeline's name as the value for the environment variable `SLUG`, i.e. `slug=oceananigans-distributed` for this pipeline).

body:
The formulation does two things:

1. it controls how the diagonals are computed and
2. it determines whether or not to apply the zero-mean gauge condition

We need to be able to modulate these independently for variations on the Poisson problem; eg problems like those enountered in an anelastic pressure correction algorithm (which modifies the z-dependent term and thus the diagnoals, but is still a homogeneous problem with a gauge condition).

cc @navidcy @giordano
title: Introduce Abstract formulation types for FourierTridiagonalPoissonSolver
